questions.explanation: put ka 3rd person form 'puts' hai.

questions.hindi: हम English mein presentations clearly present karte hain।
questions.english: We present our presentations clearly in English.
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'present' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे phone calls English mein handle karte hain।
questions.english: They handle phone calls in English.
questions.hint: They + handle + phone calls + in English
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'handle' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं अपने English teacher se daily feedback leta hun।
questions.english: I take daily feedback from my English teacher.
questions.hint: I + take + daily feedback + from my teacher
questions.explanation: I ke saath base verb 'take' aata hai.

questions.hindi: जो लोग अपनी आदतें बदलते हैं वे अपनी किस्मत बदलते हैं।
questions.english: People who change their habits change their destiny.
questions.hint: People who + change + habits + change + destiny
questions.explanation: Relative clause 'who change their habits' subject ke roop mein. Dono verbs base form mein.
questions.grammarRule: People/Those who + base verb + verb (base form)

questions.hindi: सफल लोग वही काम करते हैं जो असफल लोग नहीं करते।
questions.english: Successful people do what unsuccessful people do not do.
questions.hint: Successful people + do + what + unsuccessful people + do not do
questions.explanation: Noun clause 'what unsuccessful people do not do' object ke roop mein.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + do + what + Subject + do not + base verb

questions.hindi: सही शब्द सही समय पर कहना एक कला है।
questions.english: Saying the right word at the right time is an art.
questions.hint: Saying + the right word + at the right time + is + an art
questions.explanation: Gerund phrase 'Saying the right word' subject ke roop mein. Singular hai, isliye 'is'.

questions.hindi: जो जितना ज़्यादा सुनता है वो उतना ज़्यादा सीखता है।
questions.english: The more someone listens, the more they learn.
questions.hint: The more + someone + listens, the more + they + learn
questions.explanation: 'The more...the more' comparative structure.
questions.grammarRule: The more + subject + verb, the more + subject + verb

questions.hindi: कामयाब व्यक्ति समस्याओं में अवसर देखते हैं।
questions.english: Successful people see opportunities in problems.
questions.hint: Successful people + see + opportunities + in problems
questions.explanation: Plural subject ke saath base verb aata hai.

questions.hindi: जो आज की मेहनत को कल के लिए टालते हैं वे कभी आगे नहीं बढ़ते।
questions.english: Those who postpone today's hard work for tomorrow never progress.
questions.hint: Those who + postpone + today's work + never + progress
questions.explanation: Relative clause + 'never' negative adverb.
questions.grammarRule: Those who + base verb + never + base verb

questions.hindi: एक अच्छा नेता न केवल निर्देश देता है बल्कि खुद भी करता है।
questions.english: A good leader not only gives directions but also does it himself.
questions.hint: not only + gives + but also + does + himself
questions.explanation: 'Not only...but also' correlative conjunction. 'Himself' reflexive pronoun.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + not only + verb+s + but also + verb+s + himself/herself

questions.hindi: जब तक आप खुद पर विश्वास नहीं करते तब तक कोई भी आप पर विश्वास नहीं करेगा।
questions.english: Unless you believe in yourself, nobody will believe in you.
questions.hint: Unless + you + believe in yourself + nobody will believe
questions.explanation: 'Unless' subordinating conjunction condition batata hai.
questions.grammarRule: Unless + subject + verb + subject + will + base verb

questions.hindi: बड़ा सोचने वाला व्यक्ति बड़ी बात करता है, बड़ा काम करता है।
questions.english: A person who thinks big speaks big and does big things.
questions.hint: A person who + thinks big + speaks big + and + does big things
questions.explanation: Relative clause 'who thinks big' subject ko describe karta hai.
questions.grammarRule: A person who + verb+s + verb+s + and + verb+s

questions.hindi: जो अपनी भाषा से शर्मिंदा होता है उसे उस भाषा में महारत नहीं मिलती।
questions.english: One who is ashamed of their language never masters that language.
questions.hint: One who + is ashamed + never + masters
questions.explanation: Relative clause + 'never' negative adverb.
questions.grammarRule: One who + is + adjective + never + verb+s

questions.hindi: मेरा मानना है कि regular practice से कोई भी English fluently बोल सकता है।
questions.english: I believe that anyone can speak English fluently with regular practice.
questions.hint: I + believe + that + anyone + can speak + fluently
questions.explanation: Noun clause 'that anyone can speak...' object ke roop mein.
questions.grammarRule: I believe that + subject + can + base verb + adverb

questions.hindi: जो समय का सदुपयोग करते हैं वे जीवन का सदुपयोग करते हैं।
questions.english: Those who make good use of time make good use of life.
questions.hint: Those who + make good use of time + make good use of life
questions.explanation: Relative clause 'who make good use of time' subject ke roop mein.
questions.grammarRule: Those who + base verb + base verb + noun

questions.hindi: अनुशासन के बिना सफलता संभव नहीं है।
questions.english: Success is not possible without discipline.
questions.hint: Success + is + not + possible + without + discipline
questions.explanation: Negative sentence using 'not possible'.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + is + not + adjective + without + noun

questions.hindi: जितनी जल्दी आप English बोलना शुरू करते हैं उतनी जल्दी आप fluent बनते हैं।
questions.english: The sooner you start speaking English, the sooner you become fluent.
questions.hint: The sooner + you + start + the sooner + you + become fluent
questions.explanation: 'The sooner...the sooner' comparative structure.
questions.grammarRule: The sooner + subject + verb, the sooner + subject + verb

questions.hindi: Consistency किसी भी skill को master करने की चाबी है।
questions.english: Consistency is the key to mastering any skill.
questions.hint: Consistency + is + the key + to mastering (gerund)
questions.explanation: Singular subject 'Consistency' ke saath 'is' aata hai. 'To mastering' gerund phrase.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + is + the key + to + verb+ing

questions.hindi: हर बड़ी उपलब्धि छोटी शुरुआत से होती है।
questions.english: Every great achievement begins with a small start.
alternatives: Every great achievement starts with a small beginning.
questions.hint: Every great achievement + begins/starts + with + a small start
questions.explanation: 'Every great achievement' singular subject hai, isliye 'begins'.

questions.hindi: जो भी आज असफल होता है वह कल की सफलता की तैयारी कर रहा होता है।
questions.english: Whoever fails today is preparing for tomorrow's success.
questions.explanation: 'Whoever' indefinite pronoun. Present Continuous tense use.

questions.hindi: जो व्यक्ति खुद पर विश्वास करता है उसे दुनिया की परवाह नहीं होती।
questions.english: A person who believes in himself does not care about what the world thinks.
questions.hint: A person who + believes in himself + does not + care
questions.explanation: Relative clause + negative sentence. 'Himself' reflexive pronoun.
questions.grammarRule: A person who + verb+s + himself + does not + base verb

questions.hindi: एक सफल व्यक्ति हमेशा तीन चीज़ें करता है: सीखना, करना, और सुधारना।
questions.english: A successful person always does three things: learn, do, and improve.
questions.hint: A successful person + always + does + three things
questions.explanation: 'Always' frequency adverb. Infinitives 'learn, do, improve' objects ke roop mein.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + always + does + list of infinitives

questions.hindi: अंग्रेज़ी सीखने की यात्रा में हर कदम मायने रखता है।
questions.english: Every step matters in the journey of learning English.
questions.hint: Every step + matters + in the journey
questions.explanation: 'Every step' singular subject, isliye 'matters'.

questions.hindi: मेरा लक्ष्य error-free English बोलना है।
questions.english: My goal is to speak error-free English.
questions.explanation: Singular subject ke saath 'is' aata hai. Infinitive 'to speak' complement ke roop mein.
questions.grammarRule: My goal + is + to + base verb

questions.hindi: वह fluency ke saath accuracy bhi maintain karti hai।
questions.english: She maintains accuracy along with fluency.
questions.hint: She + maintains + accuracy + along with + fluency
questions.explanation: maintain ka 3rd person form 'maintains' hai.

questions.hindi: वह daily basis pe ek English article padhta hai।
questions.english: He reads one English article on a daily basis.
questions.hint: He + reads + one article + on a daily basis
questions.explanation: read ka 3rd person form 'reads' hai.

questions.hindi: हम professional English ke liye dedicated practice karte hain।
questions.english: We practice dedicatedly for professional English.
questions.hint: We + practice + dedicatedly + for professional English
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'practice' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे apni English mein confidence build karte hain।
questions.english: They build confidence in their English.
questions.hint: They + build + confidence + in their English
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'build' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं हर day नए idioms और phrases सीखता हूँ।
questions.english: I learn new idioms and phrases every day.
questions.hint: I + learn + new idioms and phrases + every day
questions.explanation: I ke saath base verb 'learn' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वह phrasal verbs ka naturally use karti hai।
questions.english: She uses phrasal verbs naturally.
questions.explanation: use ka 3rd person form 'uses' hai.

questions.hindi: वह English mein jokes aur stories share karta hai।
questions.english: He shares jokes and stories in English.
questions.hint: He + shares + jokes and stories + in English
questions.explanation: share ka 3rd person form 'shares' hai.

questions.hindi: हम English debate mein participate karte hain।
questions.english: We participate in English debates.
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'participate' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे English poetry bhi padhte hain।
questions.english: They also read English poetry.
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'read' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं English mein daily affirmations bolata hun।
questions.english: I say daily affirmations in English.
questions.hint: I + say + daily affirmations + in English
questions.explanation: I ke saath base verb 'say' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वह apni English learning mein discipline follow karti hai।
questions.english: She follows discipline in her English learning.
questions.explanation: follow ka 3rd person form 'follows' hai.

questions.hindi: वह English mein short stories likhta hai।
questions.english: He writes short stories in English.
questions.hint: He + writes + short stories + in English
questions.explanation: write ka 3rd person form 'writes' hai.

questions.hindi: हम English mein group discussions karte hain।
questions.english: We conduct group discussions in English.
questions.hint: We + conduct + group discussions + in English
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'conduct' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे English learning apps daily use karte hain।
questions.english: They use English learning apps daily.
questions.hint: They + use + English learning apps + daily
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'use' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं apni progress ke liye weekly self-assessment karta hun।
questions.english: I do a weekly self-assessment of my progress.
questions.explanation: I ke saath base verb 'do' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वह apni English mistakes ko record karti hai।
questions.english: She records her English mistakes.
questions.explanation: record ka 3rd person form 'records' hai.

questions.hindi: वह apni learning aur earning dono balance karta hai।
questions.english: He balances both his learning and earning.
questions.hint: He + balances + both learning and earning
questions.explanation: balance ka 3rd person form 'balances' hai.

questions.hindi: हम 75 days mein fluent English bolne ka target rakhte hain।
questions.english: We aim to speak fluent English in 75 days.
questions.hint: We + aim + to speak + fluent English + in 75 days
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'aim' + infinitive 'to speak' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे 75 Days Hard English Course se अपनी English master karte hain।
questions.english: They master their English through the 75 Days Hard English Course.
questions.hint: They + master + their English + through the course
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'master' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं पहले समझता हूँ, फिर याद करता हूँ।
questions.english: I first understand, then memorize.
questions.explanation: I ke saath dono base verbs aate hain.
questions.grammarRule: I/We/You/They + adverb + base verb + then + base verb

questions.hindi: वह concepts ko examples se connect karti hai।
questions.english: She connects concepts to examples.
questions.explanation: connect ka 3rd person form 'connects' hai.

questions.hindi: वह mistakes से नहीं, successes से भी सीखता है।
questions.english: He learns not only from mistakes but also from successes.
questions.hint: He + learns + not only from mistakes + but also from successes
questions.explanation: 'Not only...but also' correlative conjunction.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + verb+s + not only from + noun + but also from + noun

questions.hindi: हम theory और practice दोनों को equally important मानते हैं।
questions.english: We consider both theory and practice equally important.
questions.hint: We + consider + both theory and practice + equally important
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'consider' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे हर lesson ka summary अपने शब्दों में लिखते हैं।
questions.english: They write a summary of every lesson in their own words.
questions.hint: They + write + a summary + of every lesson + in their own words
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'write' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं English mein सोचने की क्षमता develop कर रहा हूँ।
questions.english: I am developing the ability to think in English.
questions.hint: I + am + developing + the ability + to think
questions.explanation: Present Continuous: I + am + verb+ing. Infinitive 'to think' ke saath.
questions.grammarRule: I + am + verb+ing + noun + to + base verb

questions.hindi: वह हर नए word के 5 sentences बनाती है।
questions.english: She makes 5 sentences for every new word.
questions.hint: She + makes + 5 sentences + for every new word
questions.explanation: make ka 3rd person form 'makes' hai.

questions.hindi: वह unknown words को dictionary से check karta hai।
questions.english: He checks unknown words in the dictionary.
questions.hint: He + checks + unknown words + in the dictionary
questions.explanation: check ka 3rd person form 'checks' hai.

questions.hindi: हम हर रात 10 new words की revision करते हैं।
questions.english: We revise 10 new words every night.
questions.hint: We + revise + 10 new words + every night
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'revise' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे English dictionary regularly use karte hain।
questions.english: They regularly use an English dictionary.
questions.hint: They + regularly + use + an English dictionary
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'use' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं English mein सपने देखने लगा हूँ।
questions.english: I have started dreaming in English.
questions.explanation: Present Perfect: I + have + past participle 'started'. 'Dreaming' gerund hai.

questions.hindi: वह बिना dictionary के नए words का अर्थ context से समझती है।
questions.english: She understands the meaning of new words from context without a dictionary.
questions.hint: She + understands + meaning + from context + without dictionary
questions.explanation: understand ka 3rd person form 'understands' hai.

questions.hindi: वह English grammar को deeply understand karta hai।
questions.english: He understands English grammar deeply.
questions.explanation: understand ka 3rd person form 'understands' hai.

questions.hindi: हम grammar ko real conversations में apply karte hain।
questions.english: We apply grammar in real conversations.
questions.hint: We + apply + grammar + in real conversations
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'apply' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे grammar rules को examples के साथ याद करते हैं।
questions.english: They memorize grammar rules with examples.
questions.hint: They + memorize + grammar rules + with examples
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'memorize' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मेरी English हर दिन बेहतर होती है।
questions.english: My English gets better every day.
questions.hint: My English + gets / improves + better + every day
questions.explanation: Singular subject ke saath 'gets' (3rd person form of 'get') aata hai.
questions.grammarRule: My + noun + verb+s + better + every day

questions.hindi: वह English learning को serious seriously leti hai।
questions.english: She takes English learning very seriously.
questions.hint: She + takes + English learning + very seriously
questions.explanation: take ka 3rd person form 'takes' hai.

questions.hindi: वह India mein English medium school mein padhta hai।
questions.english: He studies in an English medium school in India.
questions.hint: He + studies + in an English medium school + in India
questions.explanation: study ka 3rd person form 'studies' hai.

questions.hindi: हम हर exam में अंग्रेजी माध्यम में लिखते हैं।
questions.english: We write in English medium in every exam.
questions.hint: We + write + in English medium + in every exam
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'write' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे interview में English में बात करते हैं।
questions.english: They speak in English in the interview.
questions.hint: They + speak + in English + in the interview
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'speak' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं अपने career के लिए English सीख रहा हूँ।
questions.english: I am learning English for my career.
questions.hint: I + am + learning + English + for my career
questions.explanation: Present Continuous: I + am + verb+ing.

questions.hindi: वह एक professional English speaker बनना चाहती है।
questions.english: She wants to become a professional English speaker.
questions.hint: She + wants + to become + a professional speaker
questions.explanation: want ka 3rd person form 'wants' hai.

questions.hindi: वह office में English की वजह से promotion मिलता है।
questions.english: He gets promoted in the office because of English.
alternatives: He receives a promotion at the office because of English.
questions.hint: He + gets promoted + because of English
questions.explanation: get ka 3rd person form 'gets' hai.

questions.hindi: हम English speaking confidence build करते हैं।
questions.english: We build English speaking confidence.
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'build' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे global level पर अपनी English से पहचाने जाते हैं।
questions.english: They are recognized at the global level for their English.
questions.hint: They + are + recognized + at the global level
questions.explanation: Passive voice: They + are + past participle.

questions.hindi: मैं इस course के हर question का attempt करूँगा।
questions.english: I will attempt every question in this course.
questions.explanation: Future tense: I + will + base verb.

questions.hindi: वह हर रोज़ more confident बनती है।
questions.english: She becomes more confident every day.
questions.hint: She + becomes + more confident + every day
questions.explanation: become ka 3rd person form 'becomes' hai.

questions.hindi: वह हर exam clear karta hai।
questions.english: He clears every exam.
questions.explanation: clear ka 3rd person form 'clears' hai.

questions.hindi: हम English को एक lifelong skill की तरह treat करते हैं।
questions.english: We treat English as a lifelong skill.
questions.hint: We + treat + English + as a lifelong skill
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'treat' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे 75 दिनों में एक नई English life शुरू करते हैं।
questions.english: They begin a new English life in 75 days.
questions.hint: They + begin + a new English life + in 75 days
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'begin' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं अपनी बात हमेशा English में रखता हूँ।
questions.english: I always express myself in English.
alternatives: I always put my point in English.
questions.hint: I + always + express + myself + in English
questions.explanation: 'Always' frequency adverb. 'Myself' reflexive pronoun.

questions.hindi: वह English में बात करने से कभी नहीं कतराती।
questions.english: She never shies away from speaking in English.
questions.hint: She + never + shies away from + speaking
questions.explanation: 'Never' negative frequency adverb. 'Speaking' gerund hai.

questions.hindi: वह communication को अपनी superpower मानता है।
questions.english: He considers communication his superpower.
questions.explanation: consider ka 3rd person form 'considers' hai.

questions.hindi: हम हर रोज़ English में कम से कम 10 minutes बात करते हैं।
questions.english: We speak in English for at least 10 minutes every day.
questions.hint: We + speak + in English + for at least 10 minutes
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'speak' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे बड़े जोश के साथ English सीखते हैं।
questions.english: They learn English with great enthusiasm.
questions.hint: They + learn + English + with great enthusiasm
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'learn' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं 75 days का commitment लेता हूँ।
questions.english: I make a 75-day commitment.
questions.explanation: I ke saath base verb 'make' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वह एक बेहतरीन English communicator बनना चाहती है।
questions.english: She wants to become an excellent English communicator.
questions.hint: She + wants + to become + an excellent communicator
questions.explanation: want ka 3rd person form 'wants' hai.

questions.hindi: वह अपनी English journey को enjoy karta hai।
questions.english: He enjoys his English learning journey.
questions.explanation: enjoy ka 3rd person form 'enjoys' hai.

questions.hindi: हम Day 75 तक fully fluent हो जाएंगे।
questions.english: We will be fully fluent by Day 75.
questions.hint: We + will + be + fully fluent + by Day 75
questions.explanation: Future tense: We + will + be + adjective.

questions.hindi: वे इस course को complete करेंगे और English master करेंगे।
questions.english: They will complete this course and master English.
questions.explanation: Future tense: They + will + base verb.
questions.grammarRule: I/We/You/They + will + base verb + and + base verb

questions.hindi: मुझे यह task कल तक complete करना है।
questions.english: I have to complete this task by tomorrow.
questions.hint: I + have to + complete + by tomorrow
questions.explanation: 'Have to' obligation ke liye use hota hai.

questions.hindi: उसे आज office जाना है।
questions.english: She has to go to the office today.
questions.explanation: 'Has to' 3rd person singular obligation ke liye use hota hai.

questions.hindi: उसे यह काम सही से करना होगा।
questions.english: He will have to do this work properly.
questions.hint: He + will have to + do + properly
questions.explanation: 'Will have to' future obligation ke liye use hota hai.
questions.grammarRule: He/She/It + will have to + base verb

questions.hindi: हमें रोज़ कम से कम 1 घंटे पढ़ना चाहिए।
questions.english: We should study for at least one hour every day.
questions.hint: We + should + study + for at least one hour
questions.explanation: 'Should' advice ya suggestion ke liye use hota hai.

questions.hindi: तुम्हें English practice रोज़ करनी चाहिए।
questions.english: You should practice English every day.
questions.explanation: 'Should' advice ke liye use hota hai.

questions.hindi: वे meeting में समय पर पहुँचनी चाहिए थीं।
questions.english: They should have arrived at the meeting on time.
questions.hint: They + should have + arrived + on time
questions.explanation: 'Should have' past regret ya expectation ke liye use hota hai.

questions.hindi: मैं कल Office जा सकता हूँ।
questions.english: I can go to the office tomorrow.
alternatives: I am able to go to the office tomorrow.
questions.explanation: 'Can' ability ya possibility ke liye use hota hai.

questions.hindi: वह 6 भाषाएँ बोल सकती है।
questions.english: She can speak 6 languages.
alternatives: She is able to speak 6 languages.
questions.explanation: 'Can' ability ke liye use hota hai.

questions.hindi: वह यह problem अकेले solve नहीं कर सकता।
questions.english: He cannot solve this problem alone.
alternatives: He is not able to solve this problem alone.
questions.explanation: 'Cannot' inability ke liye use hota hai.

questions.hindi: क्या हम यह deadline extend कर सकते हैं?
questions.english: Can we extend this deadline?
alternatives: Is it possible to extend this deadline?
questions.explanation: 'Can' request ya possibility ke liye question mein use hota hai.

questions.hindi: मुझे लगता है कि तुम सही हो।
questions.english: I think you are right.
questions.explanation: Noun clause 'you are right' object ke roop mein.

questions.hindi: मुझे लगता है कि English सीखना आसान है।
questions.english: I think learning English is easy.
questions.hint: I + think + learning English + is easy (gerund as subject)
questions.explanation: 'Learning English' gerund phrase subject ke roop mein.
questions.grammarRule: I think + gerund phrase + is + adjective

questions.hindi: मेरे ख्याल से वह सबसे अच्छा option है।
questions.english: In my opinion, that is the best option.
questions.hint: In my opinion + that + is + the best option
questions.explanation: 'In my opinion' opinion phrase hai.
questions.grammarRule: In my opinion + subject + is + the best + noun

questions.hindi: मुझे विश्वास है कि वह यह काम कर सकती है।
questions.english: I am confident that she can do this work.
alternatives: I believe she can do this.
questions.hint: I + am + confident + that + she + can do
questions.explanation: 'I am confident that' noun clause introduce karta hai.
questions.grammarRule: I am confident that + subject + can + base verb

questions.hindi: मुझे नहीं पता कि वह कहाँ है।
questions.english: I do not know where he is.
questions.hint: I + do not + know + where + he is
questions.explanation: Embedded question 'where he is' - note: no inversion in embedded questions.
questions.grammarRule: I do not know + where + subject + verb

questions.hindi: मुझे यकीन है कि हम सफल होंगे।
questions.english: I am sure that we will succeed.
questions.hint: I + am + sure + that + we + will succeed
questions.explanation: 'I am sure that' noun clause introduce karta hai.
questions.grammarRule: I am sure that + subject + will + base verb

questions.hindi: मुझे ऐसा लगता है कि मौसम बदल रहा है।
questions.english: I feel that the weather is changing.
alternatives: It seems to me that the weather is changing.
questions.hint: I + feel + that + the weather + is changing
questions.explanation: 'Feel' + noun clause. Present Continuous 'is changing'.
questions.grammarRule: I feel that + subject + is + verb+ing

questions.hindi: वह चाहता है कि उसकी team successful हो।
questions.english: He wants his team to be successful.
questions.hint: He + wants + his team + to be + successful
questions.explanation: want ka 3rd person form 'wants' hai. Object + infinitive structure.
questions.grammarRule: He/She/It + wants + object + to + base verb

questions.hindi: मुझे उम्मीद है कि आप समझ गए होंगे।
questions.english: I hope you have understood.
alternatives: I hope that you got it.
questions.explanation: 'Hope' + noun clause. Present Perfect 'have understood'.
questions.grammarRule: I hope + subject + have/has + past participle

questions.hindi: मुझे लगता है कि हम पहले से बेहतर हैं।
questions.english: I think we are better than before.
questions.hint: I + think + we + are + better than before
questions.explanation: Comparative adjective 'better than'.
questions.grammarRule: I think + subject + are + comparative + than + noun

questions.hindi: जब भी मैं थका हुआ होता हूँ तब भी मैं पढ़ता हूँ।
questions.english: Even when I am tired, I still study.
questions.hint: Even when + I + am tired + I + still + study
questions.explanation: 'Even when' subordinating conjunction conditional sense mein.
questions.grammarRule: Even when + subject + is/am + adjective + subject + still + base verb

questions.hindi: चाहे कुछ भी हो, मैं हार नहीं मानूँगा।
questions.english: No matter what happens, I will not give up.
alternatives: Whatever happens, I will not give up.
questions.hint: No matter what + happens + I + will not + give up
questions.explanation: 'No matter what' concession phrase. Negative future.
questions.grammarRule: No matter what + verb + subject + will not + base verb

questions.hindi: भले ही यह कठिन है, मैं कोशिश करता रहूँगा।
questions.english: Even though it is difficult, I will keep trying.
questions.hint: Even though + it + is difficult + I + will + keep trying
questions.explanation: 'Even though' concession conjunction. 'Keep trying' gerund.
questions.grammarRule: Even though + subject + is + adjective + subject + will + keep + verb+ing

questions.hindi: जैसे-जैसे मैं practice करता हूँ, वैसे-वैसे मेरी English बेहतर होती है।
questions.english: As I practice more, my English gets better.
questions.hint: As + I + practice more + my English + gets better
questions.explanation: 'As' comparative conjunction. 'Gets better' ka use hota hai improvement ke liye.
questions.grammarRule: As + subject + verb + more + subject + gets + comparative adjective

questions.hindi: इस कोर्स को पूरा करने के बाद, मैं fluent English bolonga।
questions.english: After completing this course, I will speak fluent English.
questions.hint: After + completing (gerund) + I + will + speak + fluently
questions.explanation: Gerund 'completing' preposition 'after' ke baad aata hai.
questions.grammarRule: After + verb+ing + subject + will + base verb

questions.hindi: Day 1 ki practice से मेरी English journey शुरू होती है।
questions.english: My English journey begins with Day 1 practice.
questions.hint: My English journey + begins + with Day 1 practice
questions.explanation: Singular subject ke saath 'begins' (3rd person form of 'begin') aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं अंग्रेजी में बात करने के अवसर ढूंढता हूँ।
questions.english: I look for opportunities to speak in English.
questions.hint: I + look for + opportunities + to speak
questions.explanation: I ke saath base verb 'look' aata hai. Infinitive 'to speak' purpose ke liye.
questions.grammarRule: I/We/You/They + look for + noun + to + base verb

questions.hindi: वह छोटे-छोटे daily goals set करती है।
questions.english: She sets small daily goals.
questions.explanation: set ka 3rd person form 'sets' hai.

questions.hindi: वह हर achievement celebrate करता है।
questions.english: He celebrates every achievement.
questions.explanation: celebrate ka 3rd person form 'celebrates' hai.

questions.hindi: हम हर day की शुरुआत positive thoughts से करते हैं।
questions.english: We begin every day with positive thoughts.
questions.hint: We + begin + every day + with positive thoughts
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'begin' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे खुद को हर दिन एक बेहतर version बनाते हैं।
questions.english: They make themselves a better version every day.
questions.hint: They + make + themselves + a better version
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'make' aata hai. 'Themselves' reflexive pronoun.

questions.hindi: मैं अपनी English में daily improvement देखता हूँ।
questions.english: I see daily improvement in my English.
questions.hint: I + see + daily improvement + in my English
questions.explanation: I ke saath base verb 'see' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वह consistency के साथ काम करती है।
questions.english: She works with consistency.
questions.explanation: work ka 3rd person form 'works' hai.

questions.hindi: वह अंग्रेजी को एक tool की तरह use करता है।
questions.english: He uses English as a tool.
questions.hint: He + uses + English + as a tool
questions.explanation: use ka 3rd person form 'uses' hai.

questions.hindi: हम अपनी journey में एक दूसरे का साथ देते हैं।
questions.english: We support each other in our journey.
questions.hint: We + support + each other + in our journey
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'support' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे एक दूसरे को English mein correct करते हैं।
questions.english: They correct each other in English.
questions.hint: They + correct + each other + in English
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'correct' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं अपने बोले हुए sentences को mentally check करता हूँ।
questions.english: I mentally check the sentences I speak.
questions.explanation: I ke saath base verb 'check' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वह अपनी speaking speed को control karti है।
questions.english: She controls her speaking speed.
questions.explanation: control ka 3rd person form 'controls' hai.

questions.hindi: वह Indian English aur British English dono samajhta hai।
questions.english: He understands both Indian English and British English.
questions.hint: He + understands + both + Indian English and British English
questions.explanation: understand ka 3rd person form 'understands' hai.

questions.hindi: हम English ko language nahi, ek skill ki tarah treat karte hain।
questions.english: We treat English not as a language but as a skill.
questions.hint: We + treat + English + not as a language + but as a skill
questions.explanation: 'Not as...but as' contrast structure.
questions.grammarRule: I/We/You/They + treat + noun + not as + noun + but as + noun

questions.hindi: वे English fluency के लिए dedicated hain।
questions.english: They are dedicated to English fluency.
questions.hint: They + are + dedicated to + English fluency
questions.explanation: They ke saath 'are' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मैं हर sentence को ध्यान से बनाता हूँ।
questions.english: I form every sentence carefully.
questions.explanation: I ke saath base verb 'form' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वह fluency aur accuracy dono achieve karti hai।
questions.english: She achieves both fluency and accuracy.
questions.hint: She + achieves + both fluency and accuracy
questions.explanation: achieve ka 3rd person form 'achieves' hai.

questions.hindi: वह जो सोचता है वो English में express karta hai।
questions.english: He expresses what he thinks in English.
questions.hint: He + expresses + what he thinks + in English
questions.explanation: Noun clause 'what he thinks' object ke roop mein.

questions.hindi: हम English को daily life का hissa bana lete hain।
questions.english: We make English a part of our daily life.
questions.hint: We + make + English + a part of + daily life
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'make' aata hai.
questions.grammarRule: I/We/You/They + make + noun + a part of + noun

questions.hindi: वे English sirf padhne ke liye nahi, bolne ke liye seekhte hain।
questions.english: They learn English not just for reading but also for speaking.
questions.hint: They + learn + not just for reading + but also for speaking
questions.explanation: 'Not just for...but also for' correlative structure.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + verb + not just for + verb+ing + but also for + verb+ing

questions.hindi: मैं हर गलती को सुधारकर आगे बढ़ता हूँ।
questions.english: I correct every mistake and move forward.
questions.hint: I + correct + every mistake + and + move forward
questions.explanation: Compound sentence mein dono base verbs aate hain.
questions.grammarRule: I/We/You/They + base verb + and + base verb

questions.hindi: वह हर word का full meaning notes karna jaanti hai।
questions.english: She knows how to note down the full meaning of every word.
questions.hint: She + knows + how to + note down
questions.explanation: 'Knows how to' ke baad base verb aata hai.
questions.grammarRule: He/She/It + knows + how to + base verb

questions.hindi: वह grammar errors को naturally avoid karta hai।
questions.english: He naturally avoids grammar errors.
questions.explanation: avoid ka 3rd person form 'avoids' hai.

questions.hindi: हम English learning ko एक adventure की तरह enjoy karte hain।
questions.english: We enjoy English learning like an adventure.
questions.hint: We + enjoy + English learning + like an adventure
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'enjoy' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे एक ऐसी दुनिया में जीते हैं जहाँ English ज़रूरी है।
questions.english: They live in a world where English is necessary.
questions.hint: They + live + in a world + where + English is necessary
questions.explanation: Relative clause 'where English is necessary' noun 'world' ko describe karta hai.
questions.grammarRule: Subject + live in + noun + where + subject + is + adjective

questions.hindi: मैं अपनी generation की पहचान English se banana chahta hun।
questions.english: I want to build my identity in my generation through English.
questions.hint: I + want + to build + my identity + through English
questions.explanation: I ke saath base verb 'want' + infinitive 'to build'.
questions.grammarRule: I/We/You/They + want + to + base verb + noun

questions.hindi: वह अपनी English journey को document karti hai।
questions.english: She documents her English learning journey.
questions.explanation: document ka 3rd person form 'documents' hai.

questions.hindi: वह हर रात अपने English journal mein likha hai kya seekha।
questions.english: He writes in his English journal every night what he learned.
alternatives: Every night, he writes in his English journal about what he learned.
questions.hint: He + writes + in his journal + every night + what he learned
questions.explanation: Noun clause 'what he learned' object ke roop mein.
questions.grammarRule: He/She/It + verb+s + what + subject + past tense verb

questions.hindi: हम इस course ke baad differently sochte hain।
questions.english: We think differently after this course.
questions.hint: We + think + differently + after this course
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'think' aata hai.

questions.hindi: वे अपनी success story खुद लिखते हैं।
questions.english: They write their own success story.
questions.hint: They + write + their own success story
questions.explanation: They ke saath base verb 'write' aata hai.

questions.hindi: मेरी English अब पहले से बहुत बेहतर है।
questions.english: My English is now much better than before.
questions.hint: My English + is + now + much better + than before
questions.explanation: Comparative adjective 'much better than' use hota hai.
questions.grammarRule: My + noun + is + much + comparative + than + noun

questions.hindi: वह पूरे जोश के साथ English बोलती है।
questions.english: She speaks English with full enthusiasm.
questions.hint: She + speaks + English + with full enthusiasm
questions.explanation: speak ka 3rd person form 'speaks' hai.

questions.hindi: वह अपनी journey में कभी पीछे मुड़कर नहीं देखता।
questions.english: He never looks back in his journey.
alternatives: He never turns back in his journey.
questions.hint: He + never + looks back + in his journey
questions.explanation: 'Never' frequency adverb negative meaning deta hai.

questions.hindi: हम 75 Days Hard English Course ke saath apni English life badal lete hain।
questions.english: We transform our English life with the 75 Days Hard English Course.
questions.hint: We + transform + our English life + with the course
questions.explanation: We ke saath base verb 'transform' aata hai. Ye last question hai — aage badhte raho!
